title: AdwState
description: On-disk contract for agents/{adw_id}/state.json, the sole cross-language boundary between the Python adw/ pipeline and the TypeScript adw_sdlc/ engine. Canonical v1 fields mirror adw/_state.py AdwState. Unknown keys are permitted (additionalProperties) so either engine may add fields additively; the Python reader drops unknown TOP-LEVEL keys and tolerantly coerces nested finding objects, so additions must never be load-bearing for resume.

schema_version.description: Version of this contract. Bump only on a breaking change; additive fields keep the same version. Readers treat files written before versioning as version 1.

adw_id.description: 8-char hex run id; also the workspace directory name under agents/.

issue_number.description: GitHub issue number driving the run, as a string.

branch_name.description: Git branch the run works on.

base.description: Base branch for the eventual PR.

plan_file.description: Repo-relative path to the spec produced by the plan phase.

work_item.description: Provider-neutral work item metadata written additively by the TypeScript engine. Non-load-bearing for resume; v1 issue_number remains canonical for cross-language compatibility.

change_request.description: Provider-neutral change request metadata written additively by the TypeScript engine. Non-load-bearing for resume; v1 pr_number/pr_url remain canonical for cross-language compatibility.

commit_message.description: Agent-authored commit message, executed by the control plane.

pr_body.description: Agent-authored PR body, executed by the control plane.

review_findings.description: Review findings as plain objects so they survive --resume and the patch phase can still see blockers.

severity.description: Load-bearing for the patch gate: blocker findings are the ones patch_loop must fix.

completed_phases.description: Ordered list of completed phase names; drives --resume skipping.
